name: data-connect-binding
title: Firebase Data Connect binding — 4-tier shape (connector → service wrapper → component signals → template)
description: Pattern for binding catalog-emitted Angular components to Firebase Data Connect connectors. This is the structural row (#13) in the preference ledger — no upstream precedent in any contender (spartan-ng, Volt UI, ZardUI); Cena writes it from scratch. First-pass pattern; iterates against real emission attempts.

cena.whenToUse: Every component that reads or writes server-backed state. Required for E1 (Care-team messages), E2 (Profile/preferences), and any future patient-app surface that touches the Data Connect data layer.

tiers.name: Raw connector functions
tiers.consumeFrom: DataConnectService only — components do NOT import these directly
tiers.rationale: Generated SDK. Type signatures are the contract; treat as authoritative.

tiers.name: DataConnectService
tiers.shape: @Injectable({providedIn: 'root'}) — provides `this.dc` to the connector functions; offers 1:1 typed async methods (getMessages(...), sendMessage(...), getProfile(...), updatePreferences(...))
tiers.consumeFrom: PatientDataService only — components do NOT inject DataConnectService directly
tiers.rationale: Single chokepoint for DC instance configuration. If we change DC connection, only this service changes.

tiers.name: Domain-service wrapper (PatientDataService for patient app)
tiers.owner: Andrey + Cena (the catalog emits new domain methods here when needed)
tiers.shape: @Injectable({providedIn: 'root'}) — composes DataConnectService methods into higher-level domain operations (e.g., loadProfileData() runs Promise.all of profile + dietary preferences)
tiers.consumeFrom: Components — they inject this service, not the tiers below it
tiers.rationale: The catalog targets THIS tier when emitting new bindings. Adding methods here keeps cross-cutting concerns (caching, retries, error normalization, optimistic-update reconciliation) in one place — invisible to components.

tiers.name: Component signal state
tiers.owner: Cena (the catalog emits these as part of E1/E2 emission)
tiers.shape: signal<GetXData['xPath']>([]) for reads; await this.svc.xMutation() for writes
tiers.consumeFrom: Templates — via @if (xLoading()) / @for (item of xList(); track item.id)
tiers.rationale: Components own the read state as signals; mutations call up to tier 3 and refresh-on-success or roll-back-on-failure (per event-mutation pattern).

parameters.entity: Lowercase singular entity name (e.g., messages, profile)
parameters.getMethod: Method name on PatientDataService (e.g., getMessages, getProfile)
parameters.dataPath: The path on the returned data to extract the entity (e.g., 'patientMessages', 'profile')

referenceImplementations.shape: signal<Message[]>([]) + computed thread items + onSend optimistic-append + await svc.sendMessage(body)
referenceImplementations.ledgerRow13Coverage: Binds against GetMessagesData['patientMessages'] type from generated; uses svc.getMessages({limit}) and svc.sendMessage(body) — both real methods on PatientDataService

referenceImplementations.shape: signal<GetProfileData | null>(null) + multi-card sections + per-section save methods + svc.loadProfileData()/updatePreferences()/updateDeliveryNote()
referenceImplementations.ledgerRow13Coverage: Binds against GetProfileData type from generated; uses svc.loadProfileData() (composite), svc.updatePreferences(), svc.updateDeliveryNote() — all real methods

gotchas: Components inject the DOMAIN service (PatientDataService), not the raw DataConnectService. The catalog's emission MUST respect this boundary — wrong import is a structural-tier violation.
Types come from @dataconnect/generated; never hand-author duplicates. If the generated SDK doesn't expose a needed type, fix the schema, not the component.

Optimistic-update reconciliation is the responsibility of the component, not the service. The service returns Promise<void> for mutations and Promise<Data> for queries; the component decides how to merge.

Composite operations (e.g., loadProfileData composing profile + preferences) belong in the domain service. Components calling Promise.all of multiple service methods is a smell — promote to the service tier.

Error handling: domain service throws; components catch + roll back + surface (per event-mutation pattern). Do NOT swallow errors at the service tier — components need to see them for rollback semantics.

When schema changes upstream: the generated types update; the catalog's binding refs need re-verification against the new types. Tripwire `structural` row #13 covers this — plant a violation by importing a non-existent contract type to confirm the tripwire fires.

preferenceLedgerRows.name: functional DI via inject()
preferenceLedgerRows.applies: components inject the domain service via inject()

preferenceLedgerRows.name: Data Connect contract shape
preferenceLedgerRows.applies: this entire pattern — the only structural row in the ledger; this binding IS the contract

preferenceLedgerRows.name: Behavior re-expressed as signals (not IIFE)
preferenceLedgerRows.applies: data-binding state lives in signals; not behavior IIFEs

references: Andrey's anchors: cena-health-spark/patients/src/app/services/{dataconnect,patient-data}.service.ts (tiers 2+3); messages.component.ts + profile.component.ts (tier 4 reference implementations)
In-catalog cross-references: list-stream (chat-list read shape), detail-view (single-resource read shape), event-mutation (write reconciliation 4-phase template)
cena.ownership: Cena-authored 2026-05-28 — first-pass pattern; iterates against real E1/E2 emission attempts. No upstream lineage; this is the structural-row chunk the evaluation pass named as the catalog's load-bearing novel-authoring effort.
cena.iterationStatus: first-pass — the 4-tier shape + Messages/Profile reference implementations are documented but UN-TESTED via actual emission. Tested by SC #2 + SC #3 of the proving slice.
